title: Add Installed tab to Foundry rendering disk state per CLI/app (depends on InstallReceiptsVault)
description: Pairs with the `install-receipts-vault` bead. Once the third vault (InstallReceiptsVault) ships, add a new sidebar route to Foundry — `.installed` or similar — that renders per-CLI / per-app disk state: checkmark for currently-installed entities, install timestamp, version, sourcePackagePath. Distinct from the planned Fleet route (which shows the canonical registry, what *could* be installed) — Installed shows what *is* installed. Click an installed CLI → details (binary path, last-run timestamp if tracked, install date). Click a not-installed CLI → 'Install' button that calls install-cli via Process and writes a receipt to the InstallReceiptsVault. Stretch: bulk-install verb (`Install all category=developer-tools`), per-entity uninstall button, version-mismatch detection (registry says v0.2.0, receipt says v0.1.0). This is foundry's first really-interactive view — read-only fleet/discovery routes show data; Installed tab does substrate operations through the GUI. Tier 1 -> Tier 2 transition: from 'map view' to 'production controls'.
